content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-15) imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and records GPS route data, and the app description ("RunTracker maps every run with GPS") confirms location is a core feature.
Pierre: Apple's reviewers cross-check privacy policy claims against actual app behavior, and a direct contradiction like this ("we do not collect location" vs. CLLocationManager tracking routes) is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger. Update the privacy policy to accurately disclose GPS/location collection, its use for route mapping, and whether it's stored locally/iCloud only or shared, then ensure the App Privacy nutrition label and PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy reflect the same.
